marketState: neutral-to-bearish, price sitting at fair value with a storage overhang but near-term weather support
summary: Price is essentially at fair value today ($2.909 vs fair $2.917, spread only -$0.008) so there is no clean valuation edge right now. The bigger fundamental story is bearish: storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a +72 Bcf bearish build surprise, seasonality shows price already -11.2pp below its typical-year path with only a 31% chance of being higher in 4 weeks (typical move -3.1%), and daily/weekly technicals are bearish with a 50<200 EMA cross on most timeframes. Partly offsetting that, near-term weather demand (1-7D) is running +20% above the 5-year average near the top of its range (rangePos 0.89), which supports the front of the curve short-term and is consistent with the modest front>next backwardation. The 8-16D weather outlook is roughly neutral (-3.2% vs 5y), and managed money is net short but only at the 35th percentile of the last 5 years, so it is not an extreme, one-sided crowded short. Net: fade rallies over the 3-12 week horizon on the storage/seasonality overhang, but respect the hot near-term weather as short-term support rather than chasing the daily downtrend immediately.

longTerm.rationale: Storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise, seasonality gives only a 31% chance of higher prices in 4 weeks with a typical -3.1% move, and daily/weekly technicals show a bearish 50<200 EMA cross with negative MACD histograms - three aligned bearish signals for this horizon.
longTerm.invalidation: A break and hold above $3.15 (above the plus21D fair-value point of $3.02), a sharp narrowing of the storage surplus vs 5y, or managed-money short covering pushing the COT percentile toward extremes would invalidate this thesis.

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 0.5% account risk (reduced size given conflicting daily trend)
shortTerm.rationale: 1-7 day weather demand is +20% above the 5y average and near the top of its range (rangePos 0.89), price is trading essentially at fair value with the front slightly above next (mild backwardation consistent with near-term tightness), and 1h/4h MACD histograms have turned positive even though the daily trend is still bearish and RSI (34.9) is testing oversold with price below the lower daily Bollinger band.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close below $2.75, or demand_1_7D pctVs5y falling back toward/below the 5y average, would remove the near-term weather support this trade relies on.
